paper_title: Investigating Mesozoic Climate Trends and Sensitivities With a Large Ensemble of Climate Model Simulations

methods.description: Process of simulating climate conditions using the CLIMBER-3α Earth System Model of Intermediate Complexity (EMIC) to investigate Mesozoic climate trends and sensitivities.

steps.description: All simulations were performed with the CLIMBER-3α Earth System Model of Intermediate Complexity (EMIC).
steps.searchable_summary: run climate simulations using CLIMBER-3α EMIC

steps.description: The model consists of a modified version of the ocean general circulation model MOM3, a dynamic/thermodynamic sea-ice model, and a fast statistical-dynamical atmosphere model.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate ocean, sea-ice, and atmosphere models in CLIMBER-3α

steps.description: The model is run at a horizontal resolution of 3.75° × 3.75° with 24 vertical levels.
steps.searchable_summary: set model resolution to 3.75° × 3.75° with 24 vertical levels

steps.description: The main difference between CLIMBER-3α and a coupled atmosphere-ocean general circulation model (AOGCM) is the simplified atmosphere model which describes the dynamics of large-scale circulation patterns.
steps.searchable_summary: use simplified atmosphere model for large-scale circulation

steps.description: Differences between simulated and observed climatic variables under present-day boundary conditions are still broadly similar to the original version described in Montoya et al. (2005).
steps.searchable_summary: compare simulated climate variables with observed data
